titles.title: Data Scientist
titles.description: Uses statistics, programming, and machine learning to analyze large datasets, build predictive models, and turn findings into business or product recommendations. Often cleans data, experiments with features, and communicates results to stakeholders.

titles.title: Machine Learning Engineer
titles.description: Builds, deploys, and maintains machine learning systems in production, including model pipelines, APIs, and infrastructure. Focuses on making models scalable, reliable, and integrated into software products.

titles.title: Data Analytics Manager
titles.description: Leads a team of analysts to deliver reporting, dashboards, and ad hoc analysis that support business decisions. Oversees priorities, quality, stakeholder communication, and analytical standards.

titles.title: Data Science Manager
titles.description: Manages data scientists working on modeling, experimentation, and advanced analytics projects. Sets technical direction, reviews work, and aligns data science efforts with business goals.

titles.title: Data Engineer
titles.description: Designs and maintains data pipelines, databases, and ETL/ELT workflows that move data from source systems into analytics-ready platforms. Ensures data is clean, reliable, and available for analysts and data scientists.

titles.title: Data Analyst
titles.description: Collects, cleans, and analyzes data to answer business questions and create reports, dashboards, and insights. Uses SQL, spreadsheets, and visualization tools to track performance and trends.

titles.title: Data Science Analyst
titles.description: Combines analytical reporting with basic statistical or predictive modeling to support decision-making. Often prepares datasets, runs analyses, and translates results into clear business insights.

titles.title: Defense Intelligence Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes military, geopolitical, and security-related information to assess threats, capabilities, and intentions. Produces intelligence reports and briefings for defense or national security decision-makers.

titles.title: Intelligence Analyst
titles.description: Gathers and evaluates information from multiple sources to identify risks, patterns, and emerging threats. Creates assessments and reports for government, security, or corporate intelligence users.

titles.title: Business Intelligence Analyst
titles.description: Builds dashboards, reports, and KPI tracking to help leaders monitor business performance. Translates operational data into actionable insights for finance, sales, marketing, or operations teams.

titles.title: Analytics Engineer
titles.description: Transforms raw data into clean, modeled datasets for analytics and reporting, often using SQL and data transformation tools. Bridges the work of data engineering and business analytics.

titles.title: Data Engineering Manager
titles.description: Leads data engineers who build pipelines, warehouses, and data infrastructure. Manages technical priorities, system reliability, and delivery of data platforms that support analytics and machine learning.

titles.title: Data Governance Director
titles.description: Defines and oversees policies for data quality, privacy, security, lineage, and access across the organization. Ensures data is managed consistently and in compliance with internal and external requirements.

titles.title: Machine Learning Scientist
titles.description: Researches and develops advanced machine learning methods, often experimenting with novel algorithms and model architectures. Focuses on improving model performance through rigorous testing and analysis.

titles.title: Data Strategy Director
titles.description: Sets the organization’s long-term plan for how data will be collected, managed, shared, and used to create value. Aligns data investments, governance, and analytics capabilities with business objectives.

titles.title: Supply Chain Risk Analyst
titles.description: Monitors suppliers, logistics, inventory, and external events to identify risks that could disrupt the supply chain. Uses data to assess exposure and recommend mitigation actions.

titles.title: All-Source Intelligence Analyst
titles.description: Combines information from multiple intelligence sources to produce a complete assessment of a person, event, or threat. Synthesizes reports, imagery, signals, and open-source data into actionable intelligence.

titles.title: Data Analytics Specialist
titles.description: Performs detailed data analysis, reporting, and dashboarding to support specific business functions or projects. Often works on recurring metrics, trend analysis, and ad hoc requests.

titles.title: Healthcare Data Scientist
titles.description: Applies statistical modeling and machine learning to healthcare data such as claims, clinical records, or outcomes data. Helps improve patient care, operations, cost management, or clinical decision-making.

titles.title: Operations Research Analyst
titles.description: Uses mathematical modeling, optimization, and simulation to improve complex decisions and processes. Analyzes tradeoffs in areas like logistics, scheduling, staffing, and resource allocation.

titles.title: Analytics Team Lead
titles.description: Coordinates a team of analysts or analytics specialists to deliver reporting and insights on time. Balances hands-on analysis with mentoring, task planning, and stakeholder management.

titles.title: Chief Data Officer
titles.description: Owns enterprise-wide data strategy, governance, and data capabilities at the executive level. Oversees how data is managed and used across the organization to support business goals.

titles.title: Data Analytics Engineer
titles.description: Builds and maintains analytical data models, transformations, and pipelines that make data usable for reporting and analysis. Often works closely with BI and data teams to ensure trusted metrics.

titles.title: Data Management Analyst
titles.description: Supports data quality, metadata, master data, and data process improvements across systems. Helps ensure data is accurate, consistent, and properly maintained.

titles.title: Data Science Team Lead
titles.description: Leads a group of data scientists on modeling, experimentation, and advanced analytics work. Provides technical guidance, reviews methods, and coordinates project delivery.

titles.title: Data Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes how data flows through systems and identifies issues in data capture, integration, or reporting. Helps improve system processes, data accuracy, and operational efficiency.

titles.title: Data Visualization Specialist
titles.description: Creates charts, dashboards, and visual stories that make complex data easy to understand. Focuses on design, clarity, and communicating insights to nontechnical audiences.

titles.title: Geospatial Data Scientist
titles.description: Analyzes location-based data using GIS, spatial statistics, and machine learning to identify geographic patterns and trends. Works with maps, satellite data, and spatial datasets for planning or risk analysis.

titles.title: Machine Learning Analyst
titles.description: Evaluates model outputs, prepares training data, and performs analysis to support machine learning projects. Often helps test model performance and interpret results for business teams.

titles.title: Machine Learning Director
titles.description: Leads machine learning strategy, teams, and production model initiatives across the organization. Oversees model development, deployment standards, and alignment with business priorities.

titles.title: Predictive Analytics Manager
titles.description: Manages analysts or data scientists who build forecasting and predictive models. Uses data-driven predictions to support planning, targeting, and operational decisions.

titles.title: Research Data Analyst
titles.description: Prepares and analyzes research datasets, often supporting academic, scientific, or policy studies. Handles data cleaning, statistical summaries, and reporting of research findings.

titles.title: Security Analyst
titles.description: Monitors systems, logs, and alerts to detect security threats and investigate suspicious activity. Helps protect networks, data, and users by responding to incidents and recommending controls.

titles.title: Supply Chain Data Scientist
titles.description: Uses advanced analytics and machine learning to improve supply chain forecasting, inventory, logistics, and demand planning. Builds models that help reduce cost, delay, and risk.

titles.title: Workforce Analytics Director
titles.description: Leads analytics efforts focused on workforce planning, retention, hiring, and employee performance. Uses people data to guide HR strategy and organizational decisions.

titles.title: Workforce Analytics Specialist
titles.description: Analyzes workforce data to support staffing, turnover, productivity, and talent decisions. Produces reports and insights for HR and business leaders.

titles.title: AI Engineer
titles.description: Builds AI-powered applications and integrates models into software products or business systems. Works on model deployment, APIs, orchestration, and practical use of AI in production.

titles.title: AI Risk Data Scientist
titles.description: Analyzes data to identify, measure, and monitor risks associated with AI systems such as bias, drift, safety, or compliance issues. Develops metrics and models to support responsible AI oversight.

titles.title: Applied Data Scientist
titles.description: Uses data science methods to solve practical business problems, often focusing on production-ready models and measurable outcomes. Works closely with stakeholders to turn data into operational improvements.

titles.title: Clinical Data Analyst
titles.description: Works with clinical or healthcare data to support reporting, quality improvement, and operational analysis. Cleans and interprets data from medical records, trials, or care processes.

titles.title: Criminal Justice Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes crime, court, corrections, or public safety data to identify trends and support policy or operational decisions. Produces reports and dashboards for law enforcement or justice agencies.

titles.title: Cyber Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes cybersecurity-related data such as alerts, logs, and incident metrics to identify threats and trends. Supports security teams with reporting, investigations, and risk monitoring.

titles.title: Cyber Data Scientist
titles.description: Applies statistical and machine learning methods to cybersecurity data to detect anomalies, predict threats, and improve defense systems. Works with large-scale security logs and incident data.

titles.title: Data Analyst Intern
titles.description: Supports the analytics team with basic data cleaning, reporting, and dashboard updates while learning core tools and methods. Typically works on supervised, entry-level analysis tasks.

titles.title: Data Architect
titles.description: Designs the structure of data systems, including databases, warehouses, models, and integration patterns. Defines how data should be organized to support analytics, applications, and governance.

titles.title: Data Visualization Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes data and turns it into clear visual reports and dashboards for business users. Combines analytical work with chart design and storytelling to communicate insights.

titles.title: Financial Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes financial performance, budgets, forecasts, and market data to support investment or business decisions. Uses spreadsheets, SQL, and financial models to identify trends and risks.

titles.title: HR Data Scientist
titles.description: Uses statistical modeling and machine learning on HR data to improve hiring, retention, performance, and workforce planning. Helps HR teams make evidence-based decisions about talent.

titles.title: Healthcare Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes healthcare operational, clinical, or claims data to support reporting and decision-making. Often tracks quality metrics, utilization, costs, and patient outcomes.

titles.title: Junior Data Scientist
titles.description: Supports data science projects by cleaning data, building basic models, and running analyses under supervision. Typically works on well-defined tasks while developing technical skills.

titles.title: Machine Learning Evaluation Scientist
titles.description: Tests and validates machine learning models to measure accuracy, robustness, fairness, and safety. Designs evaluation methods, benchmarks, and experiments to compare model performance.

titles.title: People Analytics Data Scientist
titles.description: Uses data science techniques on employee and talent data to answer questions about hiring, retention, engagement, and performance. Builds models and analyses that inform HR strategy.

titles.title: People Analytics Director
titles.description: Leads the people analytics function and sets strategy for using workforce data to improve talent decisions. Oversees analytics projects, stakeholder relationships, and team priorities.

titles.title: People Analytics Specialist
titles.description: Analyzes employee and talent data to support HR reporting, workforce planning, and organizational insights. Produces metrics and analyses for recruiting, retention, and employee experience.

titles.title: Predictive Analytics Analyst
titles.description: Builds and interprets forecasting models to predict future outcomes such as demand, churn, or risk. Uses data to support planning and decision-making with forward-looking insights.

titles.title: Predictive Analytics Specialist
titles.description: Develops predictive models and analytical reports to forecast business outcomes and identify likely future trends. Often works on model validation, feature analysis, and stakeholder communication.

titles.title: Quantitative Analyst
titles.description: Uses mathematical and statistical models to analyze financial markets, pricing, risk, or trading strategies. Often works in banking, investment, or quantitative finance environments.

titles.title: Risk Modeling Analyst
titles.description: Builds and evaluates models that estimate financial, operational, or credit risk. Uses data and statistical methods to measure exposure and support risk management decisions.

titles.title: Software Engineer
titles.description: Designs, builds, tests, and maintains software applications and systems. Writes code to implement features, fix bugs, and improve performance and reliability.

titles.title: Statistical Analyst
titles.description: Applies statistical methods to analyze data, test hypotheses, and summarize trends. Produces reports and interpretations that support research, operations, or business decisions.

titles.title: Workforce Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes employee, staffing, and labor data to support workforce planning and HR reporting. Tracks metrics such as headcount, turnover, hiring, and productivity.
